markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify unsupported summaries/claims are blocked or marked unsupported and cannot become stable facts. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: BadCitationFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: BadCitationFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: MT-233 unsupported citation and fact-authority hardening

evidence.summary: MT-233 now types unsupported bad-citation evidence in durable context bundle item rows, keeps emitted allowed_context JSON and persisted rows aligned, backfills legacy unsupported rows from allowed_context and @UNSUPPORTED citation markers, rejects contradictory support metadata through Rust storage validation and PostgreSQL CHECK constraints, and blocks unsupported memory facts from becoming source, derived, model_suggested, or operator_approved through both Rust transition logic and a PostgreSQL trigger. HBR-STOP-001/HBR-STOP-002 and CX-503R are proven by real PostgreSQL negative fixtures; HBR-VIS-005 is not applicable because this MT changes no GUI surface.

commands.command: cargo test -j 1 --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test knowledge_documents_tests bundle_item_support_state_rejects_drift -- --nocapture
commands.result: PASS; 1/1 real PostgreSQL negative-path test. Rust storage rejects supported=true with @UNSUPPORTED citation and unsupported_reason; raw SQL insert rejects supported=true with @UNSUPPORTED via chk_knowledge_context_bundle_items_support_reason.

PASS; 1/1 real PostgreSQL test. Unsupported fact stays out of trusted graph projection; Rust API rejects Unsupported -> OperatorApproved; raw SQL rejects Unsupported -> source with the MT-233 trigger.
PASS; 1/1 real PostgreSQL retrieval scenario. Bad citation persists supported=false, unsupported_reason='span not found in index', and @UNSUPPORTED marker in both allowed_context and item rows.
PASS; 3/3 real PostgreSQL memory fact tests, including existing authority transition table coverage.
PASS; 1/1 real PostgreSQL positive-path context bundle roundtrip with the new support-state columns.
PASS; no trailing whitespace in migration files

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test knowledge_memory_projection_tests --test knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests --test knowledge_memory_facts_tests --test knowledge_documents_tests -- --nocapture
commands.result: NOT CLAIMED AS PASS; broad combined command hit the 20-minute shell timeout under Windows paging-file pressure. Follow-up controlled logs found rustc E0786/os error 1455 and the MT-233 proof set was rerun with -j 1.

evidence.summary: Initial review had failed migration backfill and support-state drift. Re-review found both blockers resolved: legacy unsupported rows are backfilled from allowed_context and @UNSUPPORTED markers, Rust and DB validation reject contradictory support state, and unsupported memory fact relabeling is blocked in both Rust and raw SQL paths. Residual uncertainty: reviewer did not run additional Cargo tests to avoid target-dir/database contention; controller ran the targeted real PostgreSQL proof set with reduced Cargo concurrency.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-233 (BadCitationFixture / unsupported-fact hardening) requires that unsupported summaries/claims are blocked from becoming stable trusted facts and that bad-citation evidence is typed durably with PostgreSQL authority. The commit delivers exactly this: real PG columns + CHECK constraint + authority-transition trigger, matching Rust storage validation and transition logic, proven by real PostgreSQL negative fixtures. Code is real, spec-aligned, and not mock-only.
findings: Impl commit 67c0b209 adds migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0321_knowledge_memory_unsupported_fact_guard.sql: real PG columns supported BOOLEAN + unsupported_reason TEXT on knowledge_context_bundle_items, a CHECK constraint chk_knowledge_context_bundle_items_support_reason tying supported state to @UNSUPPORTED citation marker + reason, plus a BEFORE UPDATE trigger trg_knowledge_memory_fact_authority_transition_guard that raises check_violation when an unsupported fact is relabeled to source/derived/operator_approved/model_suggested. This is a genuine Handshake-owned PostgreSQL resource touch.

knowledge_memory.rs:807-813 (set_memory_fact_authority_label) and the can_transition_to logic (diff at knowledge_memory.rs ~607) now restrict Unsupported -> {Deprecated, Superseded} only, matching the DB trigger. Both the Rust API path and the raw-SQL path are guarded, so the blocking behavior cannot be bypassed.

storage/knowledge.rs:5386-5470 adds Rust-side validation rejecting supported=true with @UNSUPPORTED/unsupported_reason and rejecting supported=false without a trimmed reason or without the @UNSUPPORTED marker; INSERT/SELECT updated to persist and read the two new columns. compiler.rs:147-200 emits aligned allowed_context JSON and item rows for supported/unsupported_reason, so the durable item rows and emitted JSON stay consistent.

Tests are real PostgreSQL negative fixtures, not mocks: knowledge_pg_support.rs:108 (knowledge_pg) and knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:43 (MemoryFixture::setup) connect to a real cluster, create isolated schemas, and run all migrations including 0321; raw_connection (knowledge_pg_support.rs:81) issues raw SQL INSERT/UPDATE to prove the CHECK/trigger fire at the DB layer. bundle_item_support_state_rejects_drift, unsupported_fact_cannot_be_relabeled_into_trusted_stable_graph, and retrieval_scenarios_stale_graph_no_result_and_bad_citation_are_driven exercise both Rust-API rejection and raw-SQL DB rejection.

Spec alignment: current Master Spec module 02-system-architecture.md (~lines 10986-11016, sec 10.20/MemoryPack/semantic memory) requires evidence-gating, candidate-until-approved status, and stable facts only from trusted/evidence-backed sources. Blocking unsupported (evidence-lost) facts from entering trusted/stable labels and typing bad citations as supported=false enforces that spec intent.

No deferred-live escapes: grep of knowledge_memory.rs found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/pending MT- on the required path. The MT-233 core files (knowledge_memory.rs, migration 0321) are byte-identical between impl commit 67c0b209 and HEAD; the later checkpoint c49afb7f did not regress them.

Scope/boundary: only allowed paths touched (src/backend/handshake_core, tests). No SQLite, Docker, external search engine, or external graph/RAG service. Aside (non-blocking): handoff is at READY_FOR_VALIDATION with completed_by set but status not COMPLETED, so no self-certification occurred.
evidence_checked: git diff 67c0b209 HEAD for MT-233 files (empty), git show --stat c49afb7f

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
